findings.evidence: `BaseChange.antipode_tmul` at lines 164-167 is just `simp [TensorProduct.antipode_def]`; Mathlib exposes the general definition at `Mathlib/RingTheory/HopfAlgebra/TensorProduct.lean:73-74`.
findings.fix: First add the general theorem, e.g. `TensorProduct.antipode_tmul` for `B ⊗[R] A` under Mathlib's tensor-product Hopf algebra hypotheses, and derive this base-change specialization from it if a base-change name is needed.
findings.issue: `BaseChange.antipode_tmul` proves the pure-tensor antipode formula only for `K ⊗[k] A`, but the statement is the natural tensor-product Hopf algebra simp lemma.

findings.evidence: General theorem: `TauCeti.BialgHom.map_antipode` at lines 57-61; duplicated scalar-extension proof: lines 250-258.
findings.fix: Replace the induction proof with a direct derivation from `TauCeti.BialgHom.map_antipode (map (K := K) f) x`, or drop the specialized lemma if the general simp lemma is sufficient.
findings.issue: `BaseChange.map_antipode` reproves by tensor induction a special case of the general bialgebra-hom antipode preservation theorem introduced earlier in the same file.

summary: Two antipode lemmas are still at the scalar-extension/special-case level when the same facts belong to the general Hopf/bialgebra tensor-product API or are already proved there.
